The job market's felt tight since 2022 because layoffs stayed high (your chart nails it: ~240-280K tech jobs cut yearly, 2026 projected 284K). Companies learned to run leaner post-pandemic boom, using AI to automate routine work while hiring only specialists (AI/ML, data roles up
